description: Fair cross-lab comparison scenario with fixed duration and payload size.

comparison_profile.objective: Compare cryptographic overhead against the global multi-region baseline.

workload_model.payload_shape: Same fixed-size benchmark payload as earlier labs, plus encrypted envelope processing.
workload_model.concurrency_model: Deterministic virtual users with one message every 1000 ms in the comparison run.

metrics_contract.latency: Client-observed end-to-end latency including cryptographic overhead.
metrics_contract.error_rate: Dropped messages plus DB errors divided by total processed messages.
metrics_contract.db_latency: Optional, reported only when a persistence path exists.

traceability.contract: trace_id and message_id survive signing, verification, decrypt, and broadcast.

consistency_model.notes: Security preserves message identity while adding CPU-bound processing before visibility.

failure_model.focus: Key rotation overhead, replay rejection, and decrypt/signature failures.
mechanisms: Invalid signature and decrypt failure paths

routing_strategy.policy: Same regional or local routing as the prior architecture, with crypto validation at ingress.
routing_strategy.objective: Preserve message-path semantics while exposing security cost.

observability.baseline: Prometheus metrics plus security-specific failure paths surfaced in the service.
observability.logging: Message IDs provide replay and security-failure traceability.

cost_model.dominant_axis: CPU cycles for encryption, decryption, signing, and verification.
cost_model.notes: Security overhead is primarily compute, not transport.

synthesis.scaling_inflection: Confidentiality and integrity change the system budget even when topology stays similar.
synthesis.learning_goal: Make cryptographic cost and failure semantics measurable.

comparison_standard.description: Fair cross-lab comparison scenario with fixed duration and payload size.

security_overhead.description: Benchmark end-to-end security overhead and key rotation.
